We understand the unique challenges faced by homeowners in this coastal Staten Island neighborhood and provide comprehensive treatments that deliver lasting results.

faqs.question: What are the most common pantry pests in Great Kills homes?
faqs.answer: Great Kills residents most frequently encounter Indian meal moths, saw-toothed grain beetles, flour beetles, and rice weevils. These pests infest cereals, grains, flour, pasta, dried fruits, nuts, and spices. You may notice small moths flying around your kitchen, webbing in food packages, or tiny beetles in your pantry items.

faqs.question: How quickly can you treat a pantry pest problem in Great Kills?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day or next-day service for most Great Kills locations. Treatment typically takes 1-2 hours and includes inspection, removal of infested items, targeted treatment of cracks and crevices, and preventative measures. Most infestations are resolved with one comprehensive treatment, though severe cases may require a follow-up visit included in your service.

faqs.question: What's included in the $350-$550 pantry pest control service?
faqs.answer: Our service includes a thorough inspection of all pantry areas and food storage spaces, identification of infested products, targeted application of food-safe treatments to shelving and storage areas, crack and crevice treatment, pheromone traps installation, and detailed prevention recommendations. Pricing depends on the severity of infestation and home size, with most Great Kills homes falling in the mid-range.

faqs.question: How can I prevent pantry pests from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Store all dry goods in airtight glass or heavy plastic containers, inspect groceries before bringing them home, rotate older items to the front, clean pantry shelves regularly, and keep your pantry cool and dry. We'll provide specific recommendations during service and can schedule periodic inspections for Great Kills homes prone to recurring issues due to high humidity from the nearby waterfront.
